skill.name: amazon-competitor-analyzer
skill.description: Scrapes Amazon product data from ASINs using BrowserAct API and performs competitive analysis. Compares specifications, pricing, review quality, and visual strategies to identify competitor moats and vulnerabilities.
skill.summary: Scrapes Amazon product data from ASINs and performs competitive analysis to identify market positioning.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 86 static findings across 3 files. All findings are legitimate API client patterns, not security vulnerabilities. The skill uses standard environment variable configuration for BrowserAct API credentials, makes outbound network requests to the legitimate BrowserAct service, and writes analysis reports to user-specified directories. The static scanner flagged standard practices (dotenv loading, requests library usage, argparse CLI parsing) as suspicious. No malicious intent or actual vulnerabilities confirmed after code review.

low_findings.title: Standard Environment Variable Configuration
low_findings.description: Code uses os.getenv() and python-dotenv to read API credentials from environment. This is standard practice for API client configuration. The .env.example file contains only placeholder text (YOUR_API_KEY_HERE), not actual secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by code review: os.getenv() reads environment variables which is standard practice; .env.example contains only placeholder text

low_findings.title: Network Requests to BrowserAct API
low_findings.description: Uses requests library to call api.browseract.com - the legitimate API service for browser automation. This is the intended functionality of the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code inspection confirms requests are made to the legitimate BrowserAct API endpoint for product scraping

low_findings.title: File Write Operations for Report Generation
low_findings.description: Writes CSV, Markdown, and JSON files to user-specified output directories. This is the legitimate feature of saving analysis results.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Code review confirms file writes are for saving user-requested analysis reports in standard formats

dangerous_patterns.title: Argparse Misidentified as Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged argparse.ArgumentParser() at line 342 as a 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is a false positive - argparse is a CLI argument parser, not cryptography.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct code inspection confirms argparse.ArgumentParser is used for CLI argument parsing, not cryptography

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation Command Examples Misidentified
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged SKILL.md bash command examples as 'external_commands'. These are documentation showing users how to run the skill, not actual command executions in code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md contains only markdown documentation with example commands; no exec() or shell execution in actual Python code

content.value_statement: Manual competitor research takes hours of manual copy-pasting. This skill automates Amazon product data collection from ASINs and generates comparative analysis reports in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Scrape Amazon product data (title, price, rating, reviews, specifications) from multiple ASINs
Generate comparative CSV reports with pricing and rating analysis
Create comprehensive Markdown analysis reports with market positioning
Export structured JSON data with competitive analysis metrics
Extract technical specifications and feature lists from product pages
Analyze review patterns including quantity and average ratings
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key for browser automation
Cannot bypass Amazon rate limiting or CAPTCHA systems
Analysis depth depends on data available on public Amazon pages
Results accuracy depends on Amazon page structure changes

use_cases.title: Market Research Teams
use_cases.description: E-commerce analysts use this to quickly compare pricing, ratings, and features across competing Amazon products before launching new products.

use_cases.title: Seller Consultants
use_cases.description: Amazon seller consultants analyze competitor ASINs to identify market gaps, pricing opportunities, and feature differentiators for their seller clients.

use_cases.title: Brand Analysts
use_cases.description: Brand strategists use competitive analysis from ASIN data to understand market positioning, competitor strengths, and potential vulnerabilities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the amazon-competitor-analyzer skill to compare these Amazon products: B09G9GB4MG, B07ABC11111, B08N5WRWNW. Generate a markdown report with pricing and rating analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick competitive overview of specific products

prompt_templates.title: Price Band Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run amazon-competitor-analyzer on ASINs B09G9GB4MG, B07ABC11111, B08N5WRWNW, B0Example1. Export to CSV and identify the price range and which products are priced above or below the market average.
prompt_templates.scenario: When analyzing pricing strategy across multiple products

prompt_templates.title: Market Opportunity Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Using amazon-competitor-analyzer, scrape data for these competing ASINs and generate a comprehensive analysis identifying market leaders, pricing patterns, and potential gaps we could exploit.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing for new product launch decisions

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Feature Matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use amazon-competitor-analyzer to collect specifications from these products. Then create a feature comparison matrix showing which competitor has which capabilities and identify underserved market segments.
prompt_templates.scenario: When mapping out competitive feature landscapes

output: - amazon_analysis.csv: Tabular data with ASIN, brand, price, rating, reviews
- amazon_analysis.md: Formatted report with price positioning and rating leaders
- amazon_analysis.json: Complete data with competitive positioning metrics

output_examples.input: Run competitive analysis on B09G9GB4MG and export to CSV
output: - Top Rated: Acme Brand at 4.7/5 stars
Report saved to ./amazon_analysis.csv with full product details

best_practices: Set up a dedicated BrowserAct API key for competitive research to separate from production uses
Use output directories to organize analysis by product category or time period
Run multiple analyses periodically to track competitor pricing and rating changes over time
anti_patterns: Do not run rapid consecutive analyses as this may trigger rate limiting from the BrowserAct API
Do not share or commit .env files containing your API key - keep them in gitignored locations only
Do not use scraped data for price fixing or coordinated market manipulation - this violates antitrust laws

faq.question: What is an ASIN and how do I find one?
faq.answer: ASIN stands for Amazon Standard Identification Number. It is a 10-character alphanumeric code unique to each product. Find it in Amazon product URLs (amazon.com/dp/B09G9GB4MG) or on product detail pages.

faq.question: Do I need a BrowserAct account?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill uses BrowserAct's browser automation API to scrape Amazon data. You need an account and API key from browseract.com.

faq.question: What data does the analysis include?
faq.answer: The analysis extracts product title, brand, current price, original price, discount percentage, average rating, review count, weight, and key features from Amazon product pages.

faq.question: How long does analysis take?
faq.answer: Each ASIN typically takes 30-60 seconds to scrape depending on BrowserAct API load. Multiple ASINs run sequentially with a 5-second delay between requests.

faq.question: Can I analyze private label or restricted products?
faq.answer: This skill scrapes publicly available Amazon product page data. Products with restricted visibility or behind login walls may return incomplete data.

faq.question: How often should I run competitive analysis?
faq.answer: Weekly or bi-weekly analysis is recommended for tracking pricing trends. Monthly analysis is sufficient for general competitive monitoring unless you are in a fast-moving market.
